headline: 14 contracts discovered across Merlin Chain L2 and Arbitrum; core staking/bridge infrastructure on L2, governance token on Arbitrum
short_headline: 14 contracts on Merlin L2 and Arbitrum

findings.text: Etherscan block-explorer name-tag search blocked by allowlist; Arbiscan search found 1 address: MERL token 0xe98004e836a497cf0cfe3de09c35241311466e25 on Arbitrum One

Official deployments doc located at https://docs.merlinchain.io/merlin-docs/developers/builder-guides/general-contracts/contract-addresses-mainnet; lists 3 core contracts (Multicall3, WBTC, MBTC) on Merlin Chain L2
No audit links provided in pinned context; no audit PDFs fetched
No GitHub repo pinned in protocol context; discovery limited to web sources

Protocol spans Bitcoin (L1), Merlin Chain L2, Arbitrum, and Zkfair per pinned chains. Merlin L2 contracts enumerated via docs; Arbitrum found via block explorer; Bitcoin L1 bridge contracts not discovered; Zkfair deployment not discovered
No factory contracts identified that would require child enumeration
14 discovered contracts classified: 2 core utilities (Multicall3, WBTC), 11 M-Token ERC-20/ERC-721 staking receipt contracts, 1 governance token (MERL)

All 14 admin_addresses[] entries trace to fetched URLs: https://docs.merlinchain.io (12 L2 contracts), https://arbiscan.io (1 Arbitrum contract), https://medium.com/@merlinchaincrypto (protocol semantics)

rationale.verdict: Merged Merlin Chain L2 docs + Arbitrum explorer + public Medium articles. Core staking/bridging infrastructure (M-Token suite, WBTC, MBTC) identified on Merlin L2; governance token (MERL) confirmed on Arbitrum. Ethereum L1 bridge contracts and Zkfair deployment contracts remain undiscovered despite multiple search attempts; these gaps are recorded in unknowns[].

evidence.shows: Mainnet contract addresses table on Merlin Chain L2 listing Multicall3 (0x46063722c010AF39E465d286B84936A12aFb81F0), WBTC wrapper (0xF6D226f9Dc15d9bB51182815b320D3fBE324e1bA), and MBTC M-Token (0xB880fd278198bd590252621d4CD071b1842E9Bcd)

Bridged tokens tables listing 11 M-Token ERC-20/ERC-721 contracts deployed on Merlin Chain L2: M-USDT (0x967aEC3276b63c5E2262da9641DB9dbeBB07dC0d), M-USDC (0x6b4eCAdA640F1B30dBdB68f77821A03A5f282EbE), MERL (0x5c46bFF4B38dc1EAE09C5BAc65872a1D8bc87378), plus 9 Ordinals/BRC-20 M-Token contracts and Runes contracts
MERL ERC-20 token on Arbitrum One with 1,000,000,000,000 total supply, 220 holders, verified source code showing Ownable ERC20 contract deployed at 0xe98004e836a497cf0cfe3de09c35241311466e25

Merlin's Seal event description: users deposit funds into MPC wallets managed by Cobo and partner firms; M-Tokens generated on mainnet; staking supports BTC, Bitmap, ETH, USDT, USDC, BRC20, BRC420 assets
Live staking interface shows Merlin's Seal now closed; bridge functionality visible; 0.0003 BTC service fee listed

unknowns: D1: Etherscan direct fetch blocked by allowlist; no Ethereum L1 bridge contracts or admin addresses discovered via Etherscan name-tag search
D2: Deployed contracts documentation absent for Ethereum L1, Arbitrum L1 (only MERL token found), and Zkfair chains
D4: GitHub deployment artifacts unavailable (no GitHub URL pinned); discovery limited to web-published docs
D5: Bitcoin mainnet bridge contracts not discovered; chainId Bitcoin has zero addresses surfaced from docs/explorer searches
D5: Zkfair chain deployment not discovered; chainId Zkfair has zero addresses surfaced from docs/explorer searches
D6: Factory pattern and child contract enumeration not applicable; no factory addresses identified

protocol_metadata.about: Merlin's Seal is a staking event associated with Merlin Chain, a Bitcoin Layer 2 network with EVM compatibility. Users stake Bitcoin-native assets (BTC, BRC-20, BRC-420, Bitmap), Ethereum (ETH), and stablecoins (USDT, USDC) through MPC wallets managed by Cobo and partners. Staked assets generate M-Tokens (liquid staking receipts) on Merlin's L2, enabling ecosystem participation while original assets remain locked. The event distributes 20% of the MERL governance token based on M-points accumulated during the staking period.
